meta.title: gyn

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Split from the source PDF’s combined "andr / gyn" entry: the two roots carry opposite meanings (man vs woman) and a Language Garden plant needs one root, one meaning, so they could not stay one family. andr is its sibling plant.

root.label: gyn
root.mentor_note: gyn is andr’s counterpart, and the two roots are easiest to hold in mind together.

attempt.direction_prompt: Treats children, or treats women’s health?

parts.gloss: a specialist in a field of study

context_sentences: Her gynaecologist had run the same clinic in the same building for over twenty years.
The hospital’s newest gynaecologist trained abroad before returning to open her own practice.
Colleagues quietly avoided him once it became clear he was a misogynist who never promoted a woman.
The critic called the film’s narrator a misogynist, dismissive of every female character he meets.
The textbook described the gynecoid pelvis as the shape most common among women.
Doctors classify pelvic shape into types, and the gynecoid form is the one considered most typical.

construct_options.text: Resembling or having characteristics typical of a woman
Relating to the study of ancient women’s history
Resembling characteristics typical of a man

context_sentences: The therapist traced his gynophobia back to a single frightening memory from early childhood.
Mild gynophobia, the researcher noted, is more common in the case studies than most people assume.

construct_options.text: An abnormal or irrational fear of women
An abnormal or irrational fear of men
A strong physical dislike of crowds
